creator: clefevre@cybercable.fr
text:
"David O'Brien" <obrien@freebsd.org> writes:
 
 > On Wed, Oct 18, 2000 at 01:57:26AM +0200, Cyrille Lefevre wrote:
 > > 	Makefile:
 > > 		build depenencies to texi2html and teTeX added.
 > 
 > I am not willing to make this change.  It *GREATELY* raises the barier to
 
 I *KNOW* ;^) it was just for the fun to have info files in addition to html
 files. I prefer them.
 
 > compile the port.  A seperate bzip2-doc port should be created if these
 
 I've sent those changes to the bzip2 maintainer and got a reply from him.
 so, I guess the next release integrate those changes. do you think it is
 really necessary to create a separate port which will probably be destroy
 some time in the future ?
 
 > docs are deamed to be that useful.  Why isn't the man page suffient
 > documentation?
 
 I not say that. of course the manual pages are sufficient. but, since
 there is a texinfo file (even not perfect), it is interesting to have
 corresponding info files.
 
 Cyrille.
